references.title: UI refresh
references.borrowed: Calmer chrome, tighter hierarchy, and dense worklist scanning for the consequence row lane.
references.rejected: Did not turn the consequence studio into an issue tracker or use Linear-specific inbox metaphors.

references.title: Welcome to the new Linear
references.borrowed: Reduced visual noise across headers, panels, and child-route continuity.
references.rejected: Did not copy product-marketing polish or abstract productivity styling.

references.title: Nested Layouts
references.borrowed: Persistent shell chrome and peer-route continuity without detached receipts or sub-apps.
references.rejected: Did not mirror Vercel's app architecture or project-level IA.

references.title: New dashboard navigation available
references.borrowed: Consistent section chrome and efficient navigation between peer routes.
references.rejected: Did not use a floating mobile bar or project switching patterns because the workspace shell already owns navigation law.

references.title: Data table
references.borrowed: Disciplined row density, clear toolbar-to-detail relationship, and explicit progressive disclosure of blockers.
references.rejected: Did not collapse self-care/admin consequence work into a generic table-only operations console.

references.title: Content guide
references.borrowed: Plain-spoken patient-safe wording for self-care guidance, waiting updates, and recovery messaging.
references.rejected: Avoided abstract SaaS phrasing that would blur legal consequence boundaries.

references.title: Typography
references.borrowed: Disciplined headings and readable density for dense operational summaries and patient expectation copy.
references.rejected: Did not import NHS brand styling or public-service layout patterns directly.
